benchmark: Path A — DreamerV3-style world model (discrete-latent RSSM) canary

corpus.honestBound: 25-pair synthesized mixed-outcome corpus (60% pass / 40% fail, 2 action types) — the live harness with the mock provider produced degenerate all-pass traces (no action-outcome contrast), so a mixed corpus was synthesized in the miner's expected JSONL schema to give the shift-degeneracy canary real signal. NOT live-agent traces.

verdict: HOLD (not promoted) — 4/4 seeds failed to promote
interpretation: On a 25-pair mixed-outcome corpus the discrete-latent Dreamer predictor does NOT clear the held-out bar (0.65) in 3/4 seeds (val 0.50) and in seed 1 it cleared val (0.67) but COLLAPSED on the shift split (shift 0.20, degradation 0.47 >> max 0.15) — the canonical memorizes-train / collapses-on-novel-task-families failure the shift-degeneracy canary was built to catch. trainLoss was ~0.006-0.008 across seeds (near-zero) while val stayed at/below 0.67, i.e. classic overfitting: the predictor memorized 14 training traces and failed to learn generalizable action-outcome regularities. This is a REAL NEGATIVE result and the canary correctly refused to promote. It validates the canary's design (it catches memorization) and bounds the claim: Path A is not promotable at this trace scale. Prerequisite for a meaningful re-test: a much larger LIVE harness trace corpus (hundreds of traces with genuine pass/fail + multi-action contrast), not a synthesized 25-pair set.
falsifiableSignal: The shift-degeneracy verdict is the load-bearing signal, NOT val accuracy. Seed 1 demonstrated it.
torchBackend: CPU (torch 2.12.1, CUDA unavailable on this host)
